description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves held together by three wide, dark metal hoops. The wood has a natural, slightly weathered appearance, with visible grain and subtle color variations, suggesting durability and age. The top and bottom are flat and made from the same wood as the staves, tightly fitted to prevent leakage. One side of the barrel features a circular wooden bung, likely used for filling or emptying the contents. The metal hoops are slightly rusted, adding to the rustic, utilitarian look. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments for storage, decoration, or waste collection.
description: A rustic wooden barrel with dark metal hoops and a circular wooden bung on one side, showing weathered wood grain and a sturdy, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden staves and three metal hoops, with the top surface clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the barrel and the even spacing of the metal hoops.
description_view_2: Another side view similar to view 1, with the wood grain and metal bands prominent.
description_view_3: View showing the circular wooden bung on the side, along with the metal hoops and wood texture.
